tweets.full_text:
Claude CodeとスプレッドシートでX用の連載マンガ投稿を自動化してる。ネタ帳→下書き→投稿までの流れをコード化しただけなんだけど、これが月15本→月90本になった。

今は全部の手順をnoteで無料公開してる。でも正直、あと半年もしたら同じことを「月3980円のテンプレート」とか「有料スクール」で売る人が絶対に出てくる。というか既に準備してる奴ら見かけてる。

俺がやってることって、結局スプレッドシートに「連載マンガのネタ」「キャラ設定」「次話の流れ」をぶち込んで、Claude Codeで「これを投稿文に変換して」って指示するだけなんだ。コスト0。時間15分で1週間分の投稿が完成する。

この仕組みを有料化する理由は、たぶん「有料だと信用度が上がる」「お金を払ったから実行する心理」を使いたいからだと思う。実際そうなんだけど、本当に必要なのはお金じゃなくて「スプレッドシートに何を入れるか」という設計思想だけなんだ。

今このnoteを読んでる人たちは、半年後に「月3万かかる〜」って言われても「いや、無料でいいや」って判断できる状態になってる。その差がでかい。

3,980円で販売予定だった
Claude Code × Obsidianガイドを
48時間限定で配布します。
・
AIを使ってもアフィやnote販売で伸びない原因は、
作った記事や投稿をその場限りで終わらせていることです。
・
Claude Codeで記事・投稿・商品紹介文を作り、
Obsidianでネタ、プロンプト、
導線、反応の良かった投稿を管理する。

この流れを作ると、毎回ゼロから考えずに
X投稿 → note販売 → アフィへ繋げやすくなります。

作るだけで終わらせず、
収益導線として使い回すのが大事です。

tweets.full_text:
要はClaude Codeの直接競合で、SuperGrok Heavyっていう月300ドルプランで使える。8エージェント並列ってのはClaude Codeにはない発想で、巨大リポジトリを複数エージェントが同時に設計・検索・コーディングするって構造になってる。

plan modeはClaude Codeでいうplan flagと同じ考え方で、diff形式で変更を確認できる。ここは正直似てる。

気になるのは月300ドルの価格設定で、Claude Code ProのMax Planと同じ帯域。ただGrok 4.3の実力がどこまでかは未知数で、コーディング精度の検証はこれから。AIコーディングCLI戦争、本格的に始まった感がある

ソース: https://t.co/yX0UKVn8yw

tweets.full_text:
We had two sites cancel their Claude Code subs.  One had ~80 seats another ~100.  Today.  And mind you, they haven't been hit with this, they just read about it.  Wait until they actually make the change.

The funny thing is that neither of them are doing anything "agentic".  They just want a friendly front end (which is what our app does) to put the AI and their data together.

If they don't change their policy to make a distinction between "mass bots" and custom Claude Code processes, OpenAI and Copilot are going to eat their lunch.

If they think companies are going to mass adopt Cowork or Claude Desktop they are delusional.

tweets.full_text:
The lab behind the most sophisticated AI models on Earth -> the team that built Claude 3.5 Sonnet -> released a secretive tool that turns your terminal into a sentient developer. It has access to your file system, runs tests, and fixes its own errors in real-time.

Claude Code. The Full Tutorial. From setup to memory management. 35 minutes of pure execution. 

They dropped the documentation, but almost nobody knows how to actually configure the dependencies for a production environment.

No one's talking about the Claude.md memory hack yet. 

Bookmark it before they do. Then watch the video below.

tweets.full_text:
The one software company I have slight interest in is - $FIG 

Figma raised its annual revenue forecast on Thursday, as growing adoption of its artificial intelligence tools helped ‌convert more users to paid plans. Up 10% AH

Here’s why -Bringing Claude Code workflows directly into Figma lets developers, designers, and even hobbyists capture a real, functioning UI from a browser—in production, staging, or localhost—and convert it into editable frames on the Figma canvas. 

Shared Figma canvas, design becomes a way of thinking through the product. Because captured screens are fully editable, teams can annotate decisions, conceptualize options, and better understand the goals of teammates to elevate the outcome.

More here -> https://t.co/vWTeHMser0

tweets.full_text:
Claude Pro (~$20) > 6 paid tools ($200+/month)

All thanks to slash commands in Claude Code

Now instead of a million tabs, I have one terminal that does everything itself:
> plans entire content series
> writes 10 variations of a post in parallel
> reviews scripts for engagement and clickbait
> runs daily routines and updates trends

For content creators, this completely changes the game. The most useful commands for creative work (not just coding):
/plan - full architecture for a big series
/goal - set a goal and Claude works autonomously
/batch - 10 versions of the same post at once
/review + /ultrareview - brutal script breakdown
/loop - checks trends every 30 minutes
/schedule - puts tasks on autopilot

Have you still not tried it!?

tweets.full_text: Claude Code is Farmville for 40 year old former software engineers这个比喻让我笑了一分钟但也让我想了十分钟。两者的hook确实一样：just enough progress feedback让你keep playing。AI coding最危险的不是写不出来code而是dopamine hit让你觉得在productive但其实在build没人需要的东西。validation before building这条rule在AI时代更重要了——因为build的成本趋近于0但时间成本没变

tweets.full_text: Farmville这个比喻太精准了——两者的hook一样：just enough progress feedback让你keep playing。Claude Code的terminal output就是XP bar。但key difference：Farmville的progress是虚拟的Claude Code的是real production code。问题是when do you know you're building something useful vs just enjoying watching AI write code。这个distinction越来越模糊

tweets.full_text:
Spent the day on a SQL MCP integration that reads my Outlook emails locally into #Claude, #Codex, and Claude Code. Same setup on my MacBook Pro and Mac Studio. iCloud-synced directories. Identical software.
Works flawlessly on the MacBook. Refuses to work on the Studio. Claude Desktop just… won't talk to the MCP server on one machine.
And @AnthropicAI's Claude Code? Its advice is to give up and use the terminal instead of debugging the actual issue.
Love the tools. Hate the gaslighting. 😤😠😡😠
